mapping_metadata.analyzer: Senior Test Automation Mapping Specialist Agent

test_case_mappings.test_case_description: Test case to verify the Billing Cycle Period card display and behavior on Print and Payment History page when a subscription plan is paused, including verification of complimentary pages progress bar, additional pages tracking, tooltips, and page usage information display across different printing scenarios.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Enroll the Ink printer with 50 pages plan or higher.

components_found_in_this_batch.what_it_does: Manages the complete lifecycle of Playwright browser automation including browser launch, context creation, page initialization, and cleanup. This is the foundational infrastructure needed to run any UI test.
components_found_in_this_batch.usage_example:
with PlaywrightManager() as page:
    # Test code here
    page.goto('https://app.hpinstantink.com')
components_found_in_this_batch.relevance_reason: This precondition requires browser automation to be set up before any test steps can execute. PlaywrightManager provides the browser, context, and page objects needed for all UI interactions in the test.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the Subscription has no free months info.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires API verification or backend data setup to check subscription state. No API helper methods or subscription verification utilities found in this batch. These components likely exist in API helper or test data setup modules in other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Make sure the subscription is "subscribed" status.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires API verification or backend data setup to check subscription status. No subscription status verification methods found in this batch. These components likely exist in API helper modules in other batches.

precondition_mapping.precondition_text: Click the Pause Plan button on CIT modal or "Pause Instant Ink" link to trigger the pause plan.
precondition_mapping.notes: This precondition requires page objects and action methods for clicking pause plan buttons/links. No page objects or UI interaction methods found in this batch. These components likely exist in page object modules in other batches.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Performs complete Playwright browser setup and returns a ready-to-use Page object. Starts Playwright, determines browser type based on target platform, launches browser with configured options, creates browser context with locale/viewport/logging/video settings, creates a new page, and returns the page object.
matched_methods.usage_example:
manager = PlaywrightManager()
page = manager.setup()
# Now page is ready for navigation and interaction
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This method is essential for fulfilling precondition 0 (browser setup) and is the foundation for all test execution. It must be called before any test steps can execute to create the browser page object.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Performs complete cleanup of all Playwright resources in the correct order: page, context, browser, and playwright instance. Verifies HAR and video files were saved if logging is enabled. Catches and logs exceptions during cleanup to prevent cleanup failures from crashing the test.
matched_methods.usage_example:
manager.close()
# All browser resources are now cleaned up
matched_methods.relevance_reason: This method is needed for test cleanup after all test steps complete. It ensures proper resource cleanup and is part of the test lifecycle infrastructure.

matched_methods.what_it_does: Captures a full-page screenshot. This is imported and used by scraper.py for baseline creation, but the actual implementation is in core.utils.
matched_methods.relevance_reason: Step 20 requires visual verification by capturing and comparing screenshots. This utility function can capture full-page screenshots for visual regression testing.

matched_helpers.what_it_does: Complete browser lifecycle manager that handles browser, context, and page setup and teardown. Implements context manager protocol for clean resource management.
matched_helpers.usage_example:
with PlaywrightManager() as page:
    page.goto('https://app.hpinstantink.com/print-payment-history')
    # Test steps here
matched_helpers.relevance_reason: This is the core infrastructure class that provides the browser automation foundation for the entire test. It must be used to set up the browser before any test steps can execute.

matched_constants.relevance_reason: This constant provides the default timeout value (30 seconds) for wait operations. Many test steps require waiting for elements to be visible or page loads to complete, and this constant should be used for consistent timeout configuration.
This constant defines the maximum number of retry attempts for operations that may fail transiently. Steps 10 and 12 involve print job simulation which may require retry logic if the API call fails.

Step 21 requires responsive testing across different viewport sizes. GlobalState.target_width stores the configured viewport width for the current test run and is used by PlaywrightManager to set the browser viewport.

Step 21 requires responsive testing across different viewport sizes. GlobalState.target_height stores the configured viewport height for the current test run and is used by PlaywrightManager to set the browser viewport.

step_level_mapping.step_action: Go to Print and Payment History page under HP Instant Ink.
step_level_mapping.step_expected_result: Page loads successfully

action_components_found_in_this_batch.notes: No navigation methods or page objects found in this batch. Navigation to Print and Payment History page requires page.goto() method (from Playwright Page object) and the URL constant, which should exist in page objects or configuration modules in other batches.

verification_components_found_in_this_ba.notes: No page load verification methods found in this batch. Page load verification methods like verify_page_loaded() or wait_for_page_load() likely exist in base page objects or utility modules in other batches.

test_data_components_found_in_this_batch.notes: DEFAULT_TIMEOUT constant can be used for page navigation timeout. The actual URL for Print and Payment History page is not in this batch.

test_case_mappings.batch_analysis_summary: This batch contains core framework infrastructure modules. PlaywrightManager provides the browser automation foundation needed for test execution (precondition setup and teardown). Constants and GlobalState provide configuration values like DEFAULT_TIMEOUT, MAX_RETRIES, and viewport dimensions that are used throughout test execution. The scraper module contains a reference to save_full_page_screenshot utility which is relevant for visual testing in step 20. However, this batch does NOT contain page objects, locators, action methods, verification methods, or API helpers needed for the actual test steps. Those components should exist in other batches containing page object modules, utility modules, and API helper modules. The components found in this batch are foundational infrastructure that supports test execution but are not sufficient to implement the test case steps themselves.
